examples.title: PDCA Cycle Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates how PDCA can be implemented for quality improvement
examples.code:
PROCEDURE PDCA_Iteration(problem, currentProcess)
BEGIN
 // PLAN Phase
 analyzeProblem(problem)
 identifyRootCauses()
 designImprovementActions()
 setSuccessCriteria()
 
 // DO Phase
 implementOnSmallScale(actions)
 collectData()
 documentResults()
 
 // CHECK Phase
 compareResults(successCriteria)
 analyzeGaps()
 identifyNewIssues()
 
 // ACT Phase
 IF resultsMeetCriteria THEN
 standardizeChanges()
 updateProcessDocumentation()
 ELSE
 restartPDCA(newProblem)
 END IF
 
 RETURN improvedProcess
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how a single PDCA iteration works: planning improvement actions, implementing on small scale, checking results against criteria, and acting to either standardize or restart the cycle.

examples.title: Defect Density Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate defect density for quality measurement
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CalculateDefectDensity(totalDefects, linesOfCode)
BEGIN
 // Convert to thousands of lines
 KLOC = linesOfCode / 1000
 
 // Calculate defect density
 defectDensity = totalDefects / KLOC
 
 // Determine quality level
 IF defectDensity < 1.0 THEN
 qualityLevel = "Excellent"
 ELSE IF defectDensity < 2.0 THEN
 qualityLevel = "Good"
 ELSE IF defectDensity < 5.0 THEN
 qualityLevel = "Average"
 ELSE
 qualityLevel = "Poor"
 END IF
 
 RETURN defectDensity, qualityLevel
END

// Example usage:
defectDensity, level = CalculateDefectDensity(150, 50000)
// Result: 3.0 defects/KLOC, Quality: Average
examples.explanation: This calculates defect density, a key product quality metric. Industry benchmarks typically consider less than 1 defect/KLOC as excellent, 1-5 as acceptable, and above 5 as poor quality.

examples.title: Quality Gate Check Process
examples.description: Demonstrates process quality control at project milestones
examples.code:
PROCEDURE QualityGateCheck(projectPhase, criteriaSet)
BEGIN
 results = []
 
 FOR each criterion IN criteriaSet DO
 measurement = measureCriterion(criterion)
 threshold = getThreshold(criterion)
 
 IF measurement >= threshold THEN
 status = "PASS"
 ELSE
 status = "FAIL"
 END IF
 
 results.add({criterion, measurement, threshold, status})
 END FOR
 
 // Determine gate outcome
 allPassed = TRUE
 FOR each result IN results DO
 IF result.status == "FAIL" THEN
 allPassed = FALSE
 break
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF allPassed THEN
 proceedToNextPhase()
 ELSE
 generateDefectReport(results)
 initiateCorrectiveAction()
 gateStatus = "BLOCKED"
 END IF
 
 RETURN gateStatus, results
END
examples.explanation: Quality gates are process quality checkpoints that must be passed before proceeding to the next development phase. This ensures adherence to quality standards throughout the project lifecycle.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of criteria

examples.title: Statistical Process Control (SPC) Monitor
examples.description: Example of using SPC to monitor process quality
examples.code:
# Statistical Process Control Example
import statistics

def calculate_control_limits(data, sigma=3):
 """Calculate control limits for SPC"""
 mean = statistics.mean(data)
 std_dev = statistics.stdev(data)
 
 UCL = mean + (sigma * std_dev) # Upper Control Limit
 LCL = mean - (sigma * std_dev) # Lower Control Limit
 
 return mean, UCL, LCL

def check_process_stability(measurements):
 """Check if process is in control"""
 mean, UCL, LCL = calculate_control_limits(measurements)
 
 out_of_control = []
 for i, value in enumerate(measurements):
 if value > UCL or value < LCL:
 out_of_control.append(i)
 
 if out_of_control:
 return False, out_of_control # Process needs attention
 else:
 return True, [] # Process is in control

# Example: Monitor defect rate over weeks
weekly_defects = [12, 15, 11, 14, 13, 45, 14, 12]
is_stable, outliers = check_process_stability(weekly_defects)
# Result: False, [5] - Week 6 has unusual spike (45 defects)
# This signals process variation needs investigation
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates SPC for monitoring process quality. Points outside control limits (UCL/LCL) indicate special cause variation requiring investigation. This is a core technique in Six Sigma and process quality management.
